name: remote_mcp_server_hosting_provider_benchmarker
friction: Developers choosing a hosting provider for their MCP server have no data on how response time varies by provider or by the geographic location of the caller.
input: none — this is an always-on pipeline. It runs continuously without manual input.
output: A public website that shows, for each MCP server hosting provider, the measured response time (minimum, P50, P95, P99, maximum) broken down by the geographic location of the pinger that made the call. URL: https://mcp-server-hosting-providers-benchmark.github.io/tools_list_latency_publisher/

condition_1_pinger_geographic_coverage.description: Pingers (scripts that call each MCP server and record response time) are deployed in all major regions of the world.
condition_1_pinger_geographic_coverage.pinger_platform_decision: All pingers will be migrated to Google Cloud Run. Reasons: (1) GCR covers all 7 required regions including Middle East with 44 available regions; (2) free tier is permanent (2M requests/month, no time limit); (3) eliminates dependency on a local MacBook that goes offline; (4) eliminates GitHub Actions 90-day data retention concern for pinger jobs; (5) a single CLI (gcloud) manages all pingers across all regions. GitHub remains for code storage, result data (git commits on the data branch are permanent), and GitHub Pages publishing.

current_pingers_to_migrate.name: MacBook Paris (launchd)
current_pingers_to_migrate.issue: goes offline when the Mac is turned off

current_pingers_to_migrate.name: GitHub Actions (various US cities)
current_pingers_to_migrate.issue: 90-day retention limit on GitHub Actions job data

current_pingers_to_migrate.name: GitLab CI (North Charleston SC)
current_pingers_to_migrate.issue: redundant once GCR pingers are in place

type_a_near_end_users.description: Pingers placed near human users who run MCP clients locally on their device (desktop app, CLI). One pinger per major inhabited region.
type_a_near_end_users.rationale: When the MCP client runs on the user's device, the latency the user experiences depends on how far their device is from the MCP server.

type_b_near_llm_data_centers.description: Pingers placed near the data centers of the main LLM providers (e.g. OpenAI, Anthropic, Google). List of target LLMs to be established before placing these pingers.
type_b_near_llm_data_centers.rationale: When the MCP client runs server-side (e.g. ChatGPT iOS app, Claude web, API integrations), the latency depends on how far the LLM's server is from the MCP server — not the user's device location.

condition_2_hosting_provider_coverage.description: The list of MCP server hosting providers being measured covers the main options available to developers.
condition_2_hosting_provider_coverage.methodological_rule_pinger_provider_sepa: A hosting provider cannot serve both as a benchmarked MCP server and as a pinger platform. If a provider hosts both the pinger and the MCP server being measured, the two may share internal network infrastructure, producing artificially low latency that does not reflect real-world conditions for external users. Each provider must play exactly one role: either benchmarked MCP server or pinger platform.

providers_excluded_from_benchmark.name: Fly.io
providers_excluded_from_benchmark.reason: No permanent free tier. Fly.io offers a 2-hour or 7-day trial only, after which a credit card is required to run any app. A benchmark that requires paid infrastructure cannot be reproduced freely by the community.

providers_excluded_from_benchmark.name: Replit
providers_excluded_from_benchmark.reason: Free plan published apps go offline automatically after 30 days. A benchmark requires servers that stay up permanently. Keeping a server running beyond 30 days requires a paid Core plan at $18/month.

providers_excluded_from_benchmark.name: Google Cloud Run
providers_excluded_from_benchmark.reason: Used as the pinger platform — deployed in multiple regions to measure latency of all other MCP servers. A provider cannot be both a pinger and a benchmarked MCP server: pinging its own infrastructure would produce artificially low latency due to potential internal network routing. Same rule applies to Cloudflare Workers, which is in the benchmark as an MCP server and therefore cannot be used as a pinger.

providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.name: Google Cloud Run
providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.reason: Recommended by OpenAI in their official MCP server deployment guide at https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy

providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.name: Azure Container Apps
providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.reason: Recommended by OpenAI in their official MCP server deployment guide at https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy

providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.name: Replit
providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.reason: Code-hosting platform with always-on deployments, popular among developers

providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.name: Alpic
providers_not_yet_in_benchmark.reason: Maintains a ready-to-deploy Apps SDK starter for MCP servers, recommended by OpenAI in their official deployment guide at https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy

condition_3_ping_frequency_documented.description: The ping interval and schedule are explicitly documented so that anyone reading the results knows exactly when and how often each MCP server was called.
condition_3_ping_frequency_documented.done_when: The benchmark output documents: (a) the ping interval, (b) the cron schedule, (c) the number of pingers and their regions.
condition_3_ping_frequency_documented.current_state: 30-minute relay via GitHub Actions (cron: */30 * * * *) — 12 pingers fire one at a time, one every 30 minutes, cycling through all 12 origins in 6 hours. Each origin pings independently every 6h.

when_to_use: Read this file when you need to understand what this benchmarker project produces, or to answer the question: 'Is the benchmarker complete?' — compare the current state of each condition in output_is_complete_when against reality.
when_not_to_use: To discover the individual pipeline components — read pipeline_components.json in this same repo instead.
